response:
The erroneous code fails because the variable lacks an initial value before printing. You must assign a string to variable before using it.

Create a class named ValueAssigner that sets variable to Hello world. This class ensures the variable holds data before any operation occurs.

Build a class called StringConcatenator to join variable with the word World. This new class will output Hello world World after the merge.

Develop a final class named StringComparator to check if variable equals Hello world. This class returns true when the values match exactly.

All classes must share the same variable name to maintain consistency across the project.
